title: reproducr breaking-changes database entry
description: Schema for a single breaking-change entry in the reproducr database

from_version.description: Last safe version (exclusive lower bound). Set to one patch below the first risky version.

to_version.description: Last risky version (inclusive upper bound). See version window design principles.

risk.description: high = output values change silently; medium = may error or change output; low = minor behavioural note

description.description: Plain-English explanation of the breaking change and its reproducibility impact. Should state which version introduced the change, what the old behaviour was, what the new behaviour is, and the practical consequence.

reference.description: URL to the official changelog, NEWS.md, or documentation entry

closed.description: If true, the version window is intentionally closed — the ecosystem has moved on and the staleness checker should skip this entry. Use when to_version is deliberately set low (e.g. historical base R changes).

closed_reason.description: Human-readable explanation of why the window was deliberately closed (optional, used with closed: true).
